title: Getting Started: Readiness Check
hero: Answer a few quick questions and get one clear next step before you knit.
description:
This short readiness check helps you avoid the most common early problems: jams, sticking carriages, and mystery dropped stitches.

Choose the option that matches your setup right now, and you’ll get:

  • the most likely issue (if any)

  • one practical fix

  • one clear next step

Simple and calm — so you can start with confidence.
excerpt: Find your next right step before you knit.
whyUseThisTool: A quick readiness check can save you hours of frustration. This wizard helps you confirm the few setup basics that matter most before you knit your first rows.
metaTitle: Getting Started: Readiness Check - Knit by Machine
metaDescription: A quick readiness check for machine knitters. Confirm your setup and get one clear next step before you knit.

schemaMarkup.name: Getting Started: Readiness Check
schemaMarkup.description: A quick readiness check that helps machine knitters confirm their setup before starting to knit and provides one clear next step.

step.name: Confirm the machine is stable
step.text: Make sure the machine is secured and level before you knit.

step.name: Confirm basic readiness
step.text: Check oiling and your basic tools so you are ready to troubleshoot quickly.

step.name: Confirm the yarn path
step.text: Verify threading through the mast and into the carriage or feeder.

step.name: Follow one clear next step
step.text: Based on your selection, do the recommended action before knitting.

wizardFlow.questionTitle: Where are you right now in your setup?
wizardFlow.questionSubtitle: Choose the option that sounds most like your current situation.

options.label: My machine is secure, oiled, and threaded — I’m ready to try my first rows
I think I’m ready, but I’m not sure about one small step (threading, weights, or tools)
My machine isn’t clamped/secure or the bed feels wobbly
I haven’t cleaned/oiled the machine (or I’m not sure if it needs it)
I haven’t threaded the yarn through the mast/carriage yet (or I’m not sure it’s correct)

results.explanation: Your setup sounds solid — which means you can focus on the feel of the carriage and how the stitches form.
tryThisItems: Set a comfortable tension and knit slowly for the first few passes.
Add enough weight so the fabric stays in contact with the machine.
Watch the needles: the loop should form cleanly and stay held as you move across.
results.encouragement: This is the best moment to start simple. A few calm rows will tell you a lot.

results.causeTitle: One quick check will make everything smoother
results.explanation: When you’re “almost ready,” a tiny setup detail is usually what causes the first jam or the first dropped stitch.
tryThisItems: Do a quick carriage glide pass without yarn to feel what “smooth” is.
Confirm the yarn path is clean and snag-free from cone to carriage.
Make sure your weights are balanced and not pulling only on one side.
results.encouragement: You’re close. A 60-second check now can save you 60 minutes later.

results.explanation: If the machine isn’t secure and level, the bed can shift just enough to cause skipped stitches or a stuck carriage.
tryThisItems: Clamp or secure the machine so it doesn’t move when you push the carriage.
Confirm the bed is level and not rocking on the table.
Once it’s stable, recheck the yarn path before you knit.
results.encouragement: This is common. Getting the machine stable is a one-time win that makes everything easier.

results.causeTitle: A quick oil/clean check will protect your machine
results.explanation: Dry or dirty needle beds can create drag, sticking, and inconsistent stitch formation — especially on the first try.
tryThisItems: Wipe the needle bed lightly to remove dust and old lint.
Oil where your machine manual recommends (a small amount goes a long way).
Do a few smooth carriage passes without yarn before you thread up.
results.encouragement: You’re not behind — you’re setting yourself up for a smoother first knit.

results.causeTitle: Threading needs to be confirmed before knitting
results.explanation: If the yarn path isn’t correct, the yarn won’t feed consistently — which can cause instant problems even on plain knitting.
tryThisItems: Thread the yarn through the mast in the correct order for your machine.
Confirm the yarn is seated properly in the carriage/feeder.
Look for snags: the yarn should pull smoothly with gentle tension.
results.encouragement: Once the yarn path is right, everything else gets easier to read and adjust.
